summary: Made `src/melee/ft/ftdrawcommon.c` a matching object: `configure.py` flips it from `NonMatching` to `Matching`, while the C diff reshapes fighter draw code around an inline copy of `ftDrawCommon_8008051C` and stack-local adjustments in `ftDrawCommon_800805C8` and `ftDrawCommon_80080C28`. The symbol map was also corrected for local constants and one `ftDrawCommon_804D8370` size. The PR body says `ftDrawCommon_800805C8` was a huge function that was close to a register-swap issue and hard for the permuter; there were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: `src/melee/ft/ftdrawcommon.c`: added `static inline MtxPtr ftDrawCommon_8008051C_inline(...)`, which zeroes two `Vec3` temporaries, calls `ftLib_80087074`, reads `HSD_CObjGetCurrent()->view_mtx`, initializes a matrix with `PSMTXIdentity`, writes translation components through `mtx_thing_2`, and returns the `PSMTXConcat` output matrix or `NULL`.

`ftDrawCommon_800805C8`: replaced the call to `ftDrawCommon_8008051C(gobj, sp78)` with `ftDrawCommon_8008051C_inline(gobj, &sp54, &v, sp18, sp78)`, hoisted `Mtx sp78` out of the block, added `Mtx sp18`, `Vec3 sp54`, `Vec3 v`, `_pad[12]`, and changed `PAD_STACK(0x18)` to `PAD_STACK(12)`.
`ftDrawCommon_80080C28`: added scratch `Mtx sp18`, `Vec3 sp54`, and `Vec3 v`, removed the explicit `phi_r28 = NULL`, and changed the matrix acquisition to the new inline helper.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt`: converted several address-named string/float/double symbols to local `@NNN` labels, including `ftDrawCommon_803C0EB0 -> @463`, `ftDrawCommon_804D3A94 -> @461`, `ftDrawCommon_804D3A9C -> @462`, `it_803F67A8 -> @239`, `it_804D5350 -> @237`, `it_804D5358 -> @238`, and many `it_804DCCxx` sdata2 constants.
smart_moves: Kept the real out-of-line `ftDrawCommon_8008051C` symbol intact while adding an inline variant for call sites that needed different codegen.
Used caller-owned scratch `Mtx` and `Vec3` locals in the inline helper, giving the large draw functions control over stack layout and register allocation.

Targeted declaration order, explicit padding, and `PAD_STACK` size instead of trying to brute-force a huge `ftDrawCommon_800805C8` permutation; this matches the PR body's note that the permuter struggled with the large function.
Removed a redundant `phi_r28 = NULL` in `ftDrawCommon_80080C28`, which likely avoided an unwanted store when matching the emitted code.
Paired the source-code matching work with symbol-map cleanup and the `configure.py` object-status flip, so the object could be treated as matching by the build.
decomp_lessons: When a large function is down to a small register-allocation mismatch, look for callee inlining and caller stack-frame effects before running broad permutations.
A `static inline` clone that takes explicit scratch temporaries can be a useful matching tool when the original function symbol still needs to exist separately.
Stack-slot-shaped names such as `sp78`, `sp54`, `sp18`, plus explicit `_pad` and `PAD_STACK`, are legitimate decomp tactics when type information is still weak and frame layout matters.
Do not leave anonymous compiler constants as misleading global address names; local `@NNN` symbols with correct scope, alignment, and size make the linker map more accurate.

Only flip an object from `NonMatching` to `Matching` after source and symbol data have been reconciled; this PR demonstrates that code changes and symbol-map maintenance can be part of the same match.
naming_conventions: Real functions keep the module/address form `ftDrawCommon_800xxxxx`.
The helper uses an `_inline` suffix, `ftDrawCommon_8008051C_inline`, to indicate it is not a separate original symbol.
Unknown or stack-derived locals continue to use stack-slot names such as `spAC`, `spA8`, `sp78`, `sp54`, and `sp18`.
Compiler-generated local data in `symbols.txt` is represented with `@NNN` labels and `scope:local` rather than fabricated global names like `it_804DCCB0` when evidence says it is local.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inline a small matrix-building helper into the problematic draw paths instead of changing the standalone helper function.
Pass scratch matrices/vectors into the inline helper to force caller frame layout: `Mtx sp18`, `Mtx sp78` or `sp70`, `Vec3 sp54`, and `Vec3 v`.
Hoist block-local declarations to function scope when stack allocation order matters for matching.
Use explicit padding (`u8 _pad[12]`) and adjusted `PAD_STACK(12)` to shape the stack frame.
Remove source-level initializations that would emit extra instructions not present in the target assembly.
Correct symbol sizes/scopes for local constants, including the `ftDrawCommon_804D8370` size change from `0x8` to `0x4`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice; no reviewer-specific feedback could be extracted.
follow_up_queries: static inline helper caller-owned Mtx Vec3 matching
PAD_STACK _pad Vec3 Mtx decomp matching tactic
